subject: LU-3565 mdt: a new param to allocate sequences

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

actually.. let me change that a bit, to get rid of the hack.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(4 inline comments)

I think this is not correct to create the reserve_sequence lprocfs entry on every MDT, and I suspect we will need DNE functionality in the near future anyway.
Patch Set 1: (3 inline comments)

Patch Set 1:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

IMHO, this reserve fld seems only for refreshed FS, but I do not see any validation during the reservation? Do I miss sth?
Patch Set 1: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 1: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/d2db828e-f08e-11e2-ba12-52540035b04c).  Ran 17 tests.  1 tests failed: conf-sanity.
Patch Set 1: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 1:

please, notice in current design we can't add arbitrary records into fldb as sequence space management is implemented as a range of free sequences. so every time new request comes, we just subtract number of sequences from that range. 

IOW, an user can only specify number of sequences to reserve. to be able to specify it in form A-B, an user have to learn A first. sounds like an additional complexity?

your thoughts?

Patch Set 1:

What about being able to add sequences to the end of the currently allocated range, but at least being able to specify the target index and type?

Patch Set 1:

> What about being able to add sequences to the end of the currently allocated range, but at least being able to specify the target index and type?

so, how the string should look like? <end of range>:<type>:<index> ?

strictly speaking, I'm fine with either :) just not sure what would be preferred way for HSM people..

Patch Set 1:

In my inspection comments, I'd prefer to make the input and output formats symmetrical, so it would be possible (for example) to dump the output of the fldb and then use it to restore the fldb later:

 # lctl get_param fld.srv-testfs-MDT0000.fldb
 [0x0000000200000007-0x0000000200000008):0:mdt
 [0x000000000000000c-0x0000000100000000):0:mdt
 [0x0000000200000002-0x0000000200000003):0:mdt
 [0x0000000200000400-0x0000000240000400):0:mdt

the above exists today, but it appears that this is not the master sequence server, only the MDT0000 sequence server.  To set a sequence, the same "[start-end):idx:type" format could be used.  While "start" is redundant, it would also avoid skipping large gaps if the userspace was not in-sync with the actual FLDB content:

 # lctl set_param fld.srv-$fsname-master.fldb=[0x280000400-0x2c0000400):1:mdt

Patch Set 1:

there is an issue with adding records via *.fldb. in current implementation the entry point is the sequence server which grabs some sequences from own space and inserts them into FLDB. I think we can find proper sequence server via fldb->object->site->..., but fldb and sequence server are implemented in different modules. and such an implementation would introduce cyclic dependency making impossible to load fldb module.

I can see benefits in having the same format for listing and altering FLDB, but not sure how to implement this properly...

Patch Set 1:

I'm tempted just to duplicate fldb entry under /proc/fs/lustre/seq/ctl-lustre-MDT0000 so we can list/alter FLDB there.. nasty, but I don't have a better idea at the moment. thoughts?
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

I'm not very happy with code duplication and will think how to avoid this.. the issue though is that we can't easily put this code in obdclass, for example.  I can export few functions from fld/, but that requires some refactoring of private data..

(4 inline comments)

A couple of minor style cleanups if patch is refreshed.

Does the change to the "space" tunable actually affect the on-disk data saved in the seq_ctl file?  If not, then this patch is not sufficient.  

Separately, the "space" /proc tunables are quite dangerous in their current form.  I've filed a separate bug on that, since it should be fixed on all the release branches, and can still land after feature freeze.

Patch Set 3:

Note that with my patch in http://review.whamcloud.com/7123 writing "[1-1]" to the "space" setting would fail. It should properly parse out the current "end" value, and then write '[end-end]'.

Since this patch is a priority right now, and 7123 is not, we can wait to fix this test along with 7123, unless this patch needs to be refreshed for some other reason.

parents.subject: LU-3677 mdt: Set HSM dirty open-for-write file when evicted.

commit.subject: LU-3565 mdt: a new param to allocate sequences
commit.message:
LU-3565 mdt: a new param to allocate sequences

sequence controller (aka master sequence server) exports
FLDB in proc/fs/lustre/seq/ctl-*/fldb. the same entry
can be used to modify FLDB using available sequences:
lctl set_param
  seq.ctl*.fldb="[0x0000000280000400-0x0000000280000600):0:mdt"

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I2640811c23cacb00e423fcd408318cac2ac89c6f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7027
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
